sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Fulham have shown decent form in their last 5 matches, remaining unbeaten in 4, with a mix of draws and wins. Manchester United, however, have struggled, managing only 1 loss in their last competitive match but showing inconsistency in pre-season games with draws and wins.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last 2 head-to-head matches, Manchester United have had the upper hand with a 1-0 win and a 1-1 draw. Historically, United dominate with 4 wins out of the last 5 encounters against Fulham.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Fulham rank 14th with 1 point, scoring and conceding 1 goal, showing a balanced but underwhelming start. Manchester United are in 18th with 0 points, yet to score this season, indicating offensive struggles despite creating chances.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Fulham's ability to create big chances is limited (ranked 14th), but they convert well (3rd in conversion rate). Manchester United's expected goals suggest they underperform in finishing, which could be exploited by Fulham's defense.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at Craven Cottage between Fulham and Manchester United ended in a goalless draw. Both teams showed intent, creating significant opportunities but failing to convert. The game has been competitive, with near-balanced possession and both sides generating notable chances. Neither team has managed to break the deadlock, setting the stage for an intriguing second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: Fulham have been proactive in their approach, entering the penalty area frequently and earning several corners to put pressure on Manchester United's defense. Despite missing a couple of big chances, they've shown promise in their build-up play. Manchester United, on the other hand, have had a slight edge in possession and managed to create dangerous moments, including hitting the woodwork. Their final third entries have been marginally higher, and they've won more duels overall, indicating a physical advantage. However, their finishing has been lacking, with key opportunities squandered.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum review
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating attacking momentum between the two sides. Manchester United started with a strong push, dominating the early exchanges with consistent attacking spells. Around the midway point, Fulham gained traction, enjoying a sustained period of pressure and looking threatening in the opposition half. Towards the latter stages of the half, momentum shifted back and forth, with both teams having bursts of attacking intent. Fulham ended the half on a stronger note, showing more urgency in the closing minutes.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Fulham, Joshua King has been a constant threat upfront, getting into good positions even if he missed a big chance. Ryan Sessegnon has also been influential with his creativity on the flank. On the Manchester United side, Bruno Fernandes stands out despite missing a key opportunity, as his ability to unlock defenses remains critical. Matheus Cunha has also posed problems for Fulham's backline with his movement and shot-taking ability.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Fulham seem to be focusing on exploiting the wings and delivering crosses into the box, evident from their high number of penalty area entries. Their defensive structure has held firm so far, but they need to be clinical in front of goal. Manchester United have relied on quick transitions and individual brilliance in the final third, though their defensive organization will need to tighten up to prevent Fulham from capitalizing on set pieces. A potential tactical shift in the second half could see either side introducing fresh legs to maintain intensity.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Given the chances created in the first half, the second half is likely to see goals as both teams push for a breakthrough. Manchester United's slight edge in expected goals and physical duels might give them a narrow advantage, but Fulham's home support and attacking intent could level the playing field. The game could hinge on which team capitalizes on their big chances first. A key battle will be in midfield, where controlling the tempo will be crucial for dictating play.
